title: How to start a SaaS business

segments.text: what should people consider before
thinking about adjusting their model to
incorporate a SAS component yeah I mean
a lot of people think SAS means
subscription I think that's kind of what
I'm seeing membership people like Oh I'm
a sass I'm like what are you like oh we
do membership we have a subscription for
our gym or subscription for a restaurant
I'm like that's not SAS cuz SAS as you
mentioned stands for software as a
service Not subscription as a business
model so I think almost every business
today has the opportunity for me
membership and the reason why that's a
great business to have is that it
creates predictability in your revenue
and predictability allows you to plan
and hire and build capacity in a way
that doesn't feel chaotic so I got lucky
that I started in software and that and
many ways the reason why software spoke
to me is because I grew up in so much
chaos that the predictability and the
certainty of creating software people
paid you every month for just felt
really aligned and clear right if I
create value for you every month you
reward Me by keeping paying the
membership if I don't then you leave so
I've got a clear feedback loop and if I
do a good job I get to build that
Revenue base so I think every business
should consider adding it and if they
don't know how just look around every
industry now it's almost like
everything's a subscription I mean you
look at your credit card bill and
there's probably like you know $37 $8 $3
$30 subscriptions that you're paying for
that you're like how is that a
subscription it's like you know
maintenance contract service contracts
like they'll figure out way to do it but
it just makes for a better business as a
buy what questions are you asking or
what information are you looking for
about whether or not you go for like a
monthly versus an annual subscription to
me it's more like what's the bet I want
to make on the software so like if
you're changing CRM solutions to
customer relationship management
software that's like a every seven-year
kind of bet so if you know you're going
to make a bet on the software for that
long like it's not an easily if it's
marketing technology right and it's a
click click done then you go monthly
because I would rather pay a premium
usually about 20% more to go monthly to
have it work because if it stops working
I'm not paying for another 9 months
after 3 months whereas something like
your accounting software you're not
switching accounting software every year
so you might as well just figure out hey
can I negotiate a three or fiveyear
contract and get a heavily discounted
price right if especially if you pay up
front because then you know you're going
to still be using it in 5 years so
that's that's kind of the way I look at
it is do they have the features that are
going to allow me to stay competitive
using their solution and if so am I
committed to a long term and then then I
negotiate as big of a discount if I pay
them upfront for it
